title: Setup Development Environment
description: Install dependencies and build the egg library with instrumentation

steps.step1: Install Rust toolchain: curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh -s -- -y
steps.step2: Clone egg: git clone https://github.com/egraphs-good/egg.git && cd egg && git checkout v0.9.5
steps.step4: Install Python deps: pip install numpy scikit-learn pandas matplotlib seaborn
steps.step5: Install LLVM: apt-get update && apt-get install -y llvm clang
steps.step7: Create directories: mkdir -p src data models results figures ir

output: Working environment with egg, LLVM, PolyBench

title: Prepare Benchmark Dataset
description: Compile programs and create rewrite rules

steps.step1: Select 20 PolyBench kernels for training, 6 for testing (2mm, 3mm, gemm, symm, syrk, cholesky)
steps.step2: Compile PolyBench kernels to LLVM IR using clang -O0 -S -emit-llvm
steps.step3: Create 50 rewrite rules: 25 arithmetic, 15 control flow, 10 memory
steps.step4: Write rule definitions to data/rules.json with pattern/replacement pairs
steps.step5: Verify all IR files parse correctly with LLVM opt

output: 26 LLVM IR files, 50 rewrite rules in data/rules.json

title: Preliminary Validation of Success Criteria
description: Quick validation of thresholds on subset before full experiments

steps.step1: Select 5 representative programs for quick analysis
steps.step2: Run exhaustive ES with 4GB limit, record peak memory
steps.step3: Run random selection at 50% memory budget, measure quality
steps.step4: Estimate achievable memory reduction: test 40%, 50%, 60% budgets
steps.step5: Adjust success criteria if needed, document in results/preliminary.txt

title: Baseline: LLVM -O3 and Random Selection

steps.step1: Compile all 26 programs with clang -O3, record instruction count via llvm-objdump
steps.step2: Run random rule selection with 50% memory budget on all programs
steps.step3: Use 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) for random selection
steps.step4: Record: instruction count, memory usage, rules applied

output: baseline_simple.json with LLVM-O3 and random results

title: Baseline: Exhaustive Equality Saturation
description: Unguided ES until saturation or memory limit

steps.step1: Implement exhaustive ES: round-robin rule application
steps.step3: Run on all 26 programs, record: peak memory, saturation status, instruction count
steps.step4: Use greedy extraction for final program

output: baseline_exhaustive.json - establishes optimal quality upper bound

title: Baseline: MCTS-Guided ES
description: Monte Carlo Tree Search guidance (strong baseline)

steps.step1: Implement MCTS: 50 rollouts per decision, UCT c=1.4
steps.step2: Use same 50% memory budget as LEOPARD
steps.step4: Record: compilation time, instruction count, memory usage

title: Baseline: Aurora-Style Guidance Comparison
description: Simulated Aurora approach for direct comparison

steps.step1: Implement single-layer GNN for e-graph encoding (not full RL training)
steps.step2: Use random weights to simulate untrained policy (demonstrates inference overhead)
steps.step3: Record GNN inference time per decision
steps.step4: Compare architecture complexity: GNN vs LEOPARD's MLP/GBDT
steps.step5: Document in results/aurora_comparison.txt: training cost, inference speed, model size
steps.step6: Create comparison table matching proposal Table in Section 2.2

steps.step1: Instrument egg to extract features: num_eclasses, avg_size, depth, operator distribution
steps.step2: Run epsilon-greedy exploration (epsilon=0.3) on 20 training programs
steps.step4: Collect ~500 tuples per program = 10K total training samples

title: LEOPARD: Train Rule Scorer
description: Train lightweight models and select best

steps.step2: Train MLP: 2 layers, 32 hidden units, ReLU, Adam lr=0.001, 50 epochs
steps.step3: Train GBDT: 30 trees, max_depth=4, lr=0.1 using sklearn
steps.step6: Measure inference time: target <1ms per prediction

output: Trained scorer model with validation metrics

steps.step2: Memory budget: 50% of exhaustive peak (from baseline)
steps.step3: Confidence threshold: 0.7, fallback to round-robin when low confidence
steps.step5: Run on all 26 programs with 3 seeds (42, 123, 456)
steps.step6: Record: peak memory, rules applied, instruction count, inference overhead, fallback frequency

title: Ablation: No Learned Scorer
description: Test importance of learned vs heuristic guidance

steps.step1: Replace learned scorer with simple heuristic: arithmetic > control flow > memory
steps.step3: Run on all 26 programs with 3 seeds

steps.step1: Disable fallback: always use model predictions
steps.step2: Run on 6 test programs with 3 seeds
steps.step3: Compare degradation: measure quality drop with/without fallback

steps.step1: Run LEOPARD with budgets: 30%, 50%, 70% of exhaustive peak
steps.step2: Use 6 test programs x 3 seeds = 18 runs x 3 budgets = 54 runs
steps.step3: Record instruction count at each budget level

title: Failure Mode and Degradation Analysis
description: Study how system degrades with poor predictions

steps.step2: Inject synthetic noise: perturb predictions by 10%, 25%, 50%
steps.step3: Measure quality degradation with/without graceful fallback
steps.step4: Verify: with fallback, system never worse than random
steps.step5: Categorize failures: distribution shift, complex patterns, etc.

steps.step1: On test set, record all (predicted, actual) improvement pairs
steps.step4: Time: feature extraction, model inference, total overhead
steps.step5: Verify: inference overhead <5% of total time

title: Statistical Testing and Success Criteria

steps.step1: Paired t-test and Wilcoxon test vs each baseline
steps.step3: Check criteria: >=80% speedup of exhaustive, <=60% memory, >65% accuracy, <5% overhead, graceful degradation

output: evaluation.json with statistical tests and criteria verification

title: Generate Figures and Summary
description: Create paper figures and final report

steps.step7: Table 1: Main results with geomean and std
